data.title: Santacruz Silver Mining: Multi-Asset Operational Recovery Drives Upgraded Target Price and Buy Rating
data.description:
Atrium Research analyst maintained a Buy rating on Santacruz Silver Mining today and set a price target of C$34.20.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Atrium Research has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors related to Santacruz Silver Mining’s recent operating performance and outlook. The firm highlights that Q4/25 silver-equivalent production rose 9% quarter-over-quarter to 3.74Moz AgEq, surpassing their forecast, with particularly notable operational improvement at the Bolivar mine. Additionally, several other assets, including Caballo Blanco, San Lucas, and Zimapán, exceeded internal production expectations, underscoring the resilience and diversification benefits of the company’s multi-asset portfolio.

Atrium Research also notes that while total 2025 production declined year-over-year due to the earlier flooding event at Bolivar, the mine is now on a clear recovery trajectory, with management expecting steady quarterly gains and full normalization by Q4/26. The recovery in processed ore and output at Bolivar, combined with consistent performance across the rest of the portfolio, supports a more optimistic medium-term production profile. Reflecting this improved outlook, Atrium substantially raised its target price for Santacruz Silver Mining, reinforcing the view that the shares offer attractive upside potential from current levels and warrant a Buy recommendation.

In another report released on January 24, TipRanks – OpenAI also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a C$24.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Tenaris: Undervalued Cash Returns Leader Amid Normalizing Pricing and Growing US Volumes – Buy Rating Reiterated with €20 Target
data.description:
Analyst Matthew Smith of Bank of America Securities reiterated a Buy rating on Tenaris, boosting the price target to €20.00.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Matthew Smith has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that highlight Tenaris’s resilience and attractive shareholder returns. Despite anticipating further declines in tube pricing and a softer near-term outlook versus consensus, he views the stock as undervalued relative to the company’s still-elevated profitability. He emphasizes Tenaris’s ability to sustain a double-digit distribution yield, which he believes clearly differentiates it from both sector peers and the broader oilfield services universe. In his view, robust drilling activity, particularly in the US following a rebasing in 3Q25, supports healthier volumes even as pricing normalizes.

Smith also points to several catalysts that, in his assessment, should support the upside case. He expects Tenaris’s 4Q25 EBITDA to come in modestly ahead of market expectations and sees the company’s anticipated share buyback—around $1.2bn, significantly higher than consensus—as a key driver for the share price once announced. While acknowledging consensus forecasts for 2026 are still too optimistic on pricing, he believes the international market’s recovery in activity and margins in the second half of 2026 can underpin longer-term growth. Incorporating lower pricing but stronger US volumes, he has raised his price objective to €20, reinforcing his conviction that the risk-reward profile justifies a Buy recommendation.

According to TipRanks, Smith is an analyst with an average return of -5.4% and a 42.86% success rate.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
